results.title: Crossroads
results.creator_credit: Walter Hill, Mark Carliner and Columbia Pictures
results.brief_description: Long ago, Willie Brown bargained his soul with the Devil at the crossroads in return for fame. Gifted young guitarist Eugene (Ralph Maccio) will do anything to play the blues like his legendary hero and travels across country where he meets the now old and cantankerous Willie. Together they journey to the fated crossroads and Eugene learns that to really play the blues, you must live them first. Joe Seneca and Jami Gertz also star in this production which features an incredible soundtrack by Ry Cooder.

results.title: Love and anarchy = Film d'amore e d'anarchia
results.brief_description: In the chaotic Italy of the 1930s, Tunin (Giancarlo Giannini) is a young peasant radical who goes to the big city in order to assassinate Mussolini. His underground contact is Salome (Mariangela Melato) who is a Madame of a brothel. As in all her film work, Wertmuller combines sexual comedy with a sharp and subversive reworking of socialist and feminist themes. As the film progresses and we see the interaction between prostitutes, anarchists and Fascists, we become less sure of what is real and what is merely artiface in the world of politics. Wertmuller has pioneered a cinema that reveals politics is as much a style as it is didactism, as much performance as it is commitment. The poor foolish Tunin, who believes in love and justice, is the archetype of the Fool. The music is by Nino Rota. Winner of a prize at the 1973 Cannes Film Festival.

results.title: The Remains of the day
results.creator_credit: James Ivory, Ismail Merchant, John Calley, Mike Nichols, Columbia and Merchant Ivory Productions
results.brief_description: Kazuo Ishiguro’s novel is lovingly brought to the screen in this lavish and finely crafted study of English class relations. Anthony Hopkins, in a stunning Academy Award nominated performance, is a butler who has been a gentleman’s gentleman: propriety and service are everything. Even his master’s sympathies for the emerging Nazi Party in Germany do not affect his work. However, on looking back at his life he decides that he was mistaken to choose his work over his emotional attachment to the only woman he has loved (Emma Thompson). Slipping back and forth from the late 1950s to the late 1930s, “Remains of the Day” is both an affecting romance and a sharp and sometimes very sad, very touching examination of how the British class system trapped people into a betrayal of their individual aspirations. It is also a study of how the emerging politics of the Cold War were to finally irrevocably alter those class relations, as America rather than Europe was to dominate as a world power. Co-starring Christopher Reeve and James Fox.

results.title: Maurice
results.creator_credit: James Ivory, Ismail Merchant, Cinecan, Film Four International and Merchant Ivory Productions
results.brief_description: E. M. Forster ordered that his novel “Maurice” was not to be published until well after his death. Maurice (James Wilby) is a young law student destined for the privileges and security of his class. While at University Maurice begins a passionate love affair with the classics loving Clive (Hugh Grant). In the strict Platonic codes of the University, homosexuality is tolerated but once Clive and Maurice graduate the demands of bourgeois life mean that they must finish their love affair. While Clive recants his earlier love, Maurice finds it increasingly difficult to hide his homosexuality. Meeting the working-class stable boy Scudder (Rupert Graves), he finally rejects the Victorian morality of the bourgeoisie and makes a decision for individual freedom. Lush, elegant and masterfully directed by James Ivory, “Maurice” is an intricate study of not only the emergence of homosexual consciousness but also of the confining strictures of the English class structure.

results.title: All about Eve
results.creator_credit: Joseph L Mankiewicz, Darryl F. Zanuck and Twentieth Century-Fox Film Corporation
results.brief_description: Possibly one of the wittiest and sophisticated films ever made by Hollywood, “All About Eve” is a bitchy and sharp comedy about the jealousies and rivalries of theatrical life. Bette Davis, in one of her greatest performances, is Margo Channing, a self-obsessed, driven and committed actress who is shadowed and wooed by an aspiring starlet, Eve Harrington (Anne Baxter). However, the shy Eve is not as naive as she appears and by the end of the film she is moving to replace Margo and the poisonous cycle of ego and ambition is about to repeat itself. Wonderful adult dialogue, great direction and a cast of excellent supporting actors at their peak (George Sanders, Thelma Ritter, Celeste Holm) makes this movie one of the most entertaining ever produced. Watch out for Marilyn Monroe in an early cameo. Winner of six Academy Awards including Best Picture. Costumes by Edith Head. When they talk about the Golden Years of Hollywood this is one of the films to which they refer.

results.title: Blood and sand
results.creator_credit: Fred Niblo, Jesse L Lasky and Paramount Pictures
results.brief_description: Rudolph Valentino is Juan Gallardo, a poor boy who realises his ambition to become one of Spain’s greatest matadors. When he marries his childhood sweetheart it seems as if his dreams will be fulfilled, but he soon falls prey to a seductress. Ultimately, Gallardo is defeated as much by his own conduct as by the events in the arena. This Valentino feature prompted a series of films about bullfighters, however it is this film which sees Valentino at his “scorching” best.

results.title: The Hunchback of Notre Dame (Silent)
results.creator_credit: Wallace Worsley, Carl Laemmle, Irving G. Thalberg and Universal Pictures
results.brief_description: This classic silent film of the Victor Hugo novel is a masterpiece of film-making. Lon Chaney as the hunchback Quasimodo was grotesque and pathetic. The actor suffered for his art in a rubber suit weighing over seventy pounds. Dubbed “The man of a thousand faces”, Chaney’s portrayal of Quasimodo is not the only outstanding feature of this production. The set which took a year to build, the enormous cast of extras, adds to the epic grandeur of the film. Starring Brandon Hurst, Nigel De Brulier and Patsy Ruth Miller as Esmeralda.

results.title: The Trojan women
results.brief_description: A joint Greek and American production based on Euripedes’ play “The Trojan women”. Katharine Hepburn is Hecube, the wife of King Priam; Vanessa Redgrave is Andromache, the widow of Hector; Genevieve Bujold is Cassandra, the cursed prophetess of Apollo and Trojan Princess and Irene Papas is the beautiful Helen of Troy. The victors of the Trojan war ransack the city and take as part of their spoils, the women and children. The play tells of their bitter struggle to survive the cruelty of defeat and capture.

results.title: Serpico
results.creator_credit: Sidney Lumet, Martin Bregman and Columbia Pictures
results.brief_description: Al Pacino gives one of the screen’s great performances as Frank Serpico, a young New York cop, who is appalled by the bribery and corruption in Police Departments. Serpico refuses the many bribes which are offered him and in doing so finds himself disowned and outlawed by his colleagues to such a degree that their hostility almost costs him his life. His lone fight against corruption ultimately leads to a full-scale enquiry into police crime. Based on the book by Waldo Salt.

results.title: Little Women
results.creator_credit: Gillian Armstrong, Denise Di Novi and Columbia Pictures
results.brief_description: A glowing depiction of the true nature of family, the search for self and feminine independence based on the classic novel by Louisa May Alcott. Gillian Armstrong’s compelling film captures the romantic dramas and adventures of Mrs March and her four “little women”.

constellations_other.name: Brilliant careers (Gillian Armstrong)
constellations_other.description: Gillian Armstrong's feature film debut not only helped launch her own acclaimed career, but those of the actors as well.

results.creator_credit_for_label: Gillian Armstrong, Denise Di Novi and Columbia Pictures
results.description: A glowing depiction of the true nature of family, the search for self and feminine independence based on the classic novel by Louisa May Alcott. Gillian Armstrong’s compelling film captures the romantic dramas and adventures of Mrs March (Susan Sarandon) and her four “little women”: spirited Jo (Winona Ryder), beautiful Meg (Trini Alvarado), fragile Beth (Claire Danes) and romantic Amy (Kirsten Dunst).
